This file parses each city photo database CSV, handles comma parsing inside `{}` list
fields, adds season labels, and writes one combined cleaned output.

```{r}

library(dplyr)
library(lubridate)

```

```{r}

input_path <- "../Input Data/"

# Cities expected to have photo database exports in `input_path`.
cities <- c(
  "AUTX",
  "BOMA",
  "CHIL",
  "ICIA",
  "ININ",
  "JAMS",
  "PACA",
  "SLMO",
  "TAWA",
  "URIL"
)

```

```{r}

# Split CSV fields while preserving commas inside {...} list-style fields.
split_outside_list <- function(line) {
  fields <- c()
  buffer <- ""
  brace_level <- 0

  for (i in seq_len(nchar(line))) {
    char <- substr(line, i, i)

    if (char == "{") {
      brace_level <- brace_level + 1
    } else if (char == "}") {
      brace_level <- brace_level - 1
    }

    if (char == "," && brace_level == 0) {
      fields <- c(fields, buffer)
      buffer <- ""
    } else {
      buffer <- paste0(buffer, char)
    }
  }

  c(fields, buffer)
}

season_dates <- data.frame(
  Season = 1:41,
  start_date = as.Date(c("2015-12-18",
                         "2016-03-18", "2016-06-17", "2016-09-17", "2016-12-18",
                         "2017-03-18", "2017-06-17", "2017-09-17", "2017-12-18",
                         "2018-03-18", "2018-06-17", "2018-09-17", "2018-12-18",
                         "2019-03-18", "2019-06-17", "2019-09-17", "2019-12-18",
                         "2020-03-18", "2020-06-17", "2020-09-17", "2020-12-18",
                         "2021-03-18", "2021-06-17", "2021-09-17", "2021-12-18",
                         "2022-03-18", "2022-06-17", "2022-09-17", "2022-12-18",
                         "2023-03-18", "2023-06-17", "2023-09-17", "2023-12-18",
                         "2024-03-18", "2024-06-17", "2024-09-17", "2024-12-18",
                         "2025-03-18", "2025-06-17", "2025-09-17", "2025-12-18")),
  end_date = as.Date(c("2016-02-14", "2016-05-14", "2016-08-14", "2016-11-14",
                       "2017-02-14", "2017-05-14", "2017-08-14", "2017-11-14",
                       "2018-02-14", "2018-05-14", "2018-08-14", "2018-11-14",
                       "2019-02-13", "2019-05-14", "2019-08-14", "2019-11-14",
                       "2020-02-13", "2020-05-14", "2020-08-14", "2020-11-14",
                       "2021-02-13", "2021-05-14", "2021-08-14", "2021-11-14",
                       "2022-02-13", "2022-05-14", "2022-08-14", "2022-11-14",
                       "2023-02-13", "2023-05-14", "2023-08-14", "2023-11-14",
                       "2024-02-13", "2024-05-14", "2024-08-14", "2024-11-14",
                       "2025-02-13", "2025-05-14", "2025-08-14", "2025-11-14",
                       "2026-02-13"))
)

# Map each observation date to the seasonal index used downstream.
get_season <- function(timestamp) {
  for (i in seq_len(nrow(season_dates))) {
    if (timestamp >= season_dates$start_date[i] && timestamp <= season_dates$end_date[i]) {
      return(season_dates$Season[i])
    }
  }
  return(NA)
}

```

```{r}

# Parse one city file and keep only occupancy-relevant columns.
clean_city <- function(city) {
  file_path <- paste0(input_path, "photo_database_fin_", city, ".csv")

  lines <- readLines(file_path)

  # First line is header; remaining lines are parsed row-wise.
  header <- strsplit(lines[1], ",")[[1]]
  header <- gsub("^\"|\"$", "", header)
  parsed <- do.call(rbind, lapply(lines[-1], split_outside_list))
  parsed <- as.data.frame(parsed, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  colnames(parsed) <- header

  # Keep only fields needed in occupancy and richness workflows.
  keep <- c("locAbbr", "Timestamp", "commonName", "maxDetectionConf")
  parsed <- parsed[keep]
  parsed <- parsed[parsed$Timestamp != "NA", ]

  parsed$locAbbr <- gsub("^\"|\"$", "", parsed$locAbbr)
  parsed$Timestamp <- gsub("^\"|\"$", "", parsed$Timestamp)
  parsed$commonName <- gsub("^\"|\"$", "", parsed$commonName)
  parsed$City <- city

  # Convert timestamps to Date and attach standardized season values.
  parsed <- parsed %>%
    mutate(
      Timestamp = as.Date(Timestamp),
      Season = sapply(Timestamp, get_season)
    )

  parsed
}

```

```{r}

city_dfs <- lapply(cities, clean_city)

# Bind one standardized table across all cities.
multi_city_data_cleaned <- bind_rows(city_dfs)

# Combined cleaned file consumed by occupancy/richness scripts.
write.csv(
  multi_city_data_cleaned,
  paste0(input_path, "multi_city_data_cleaned.csv"),
  row.names = FALSE
)

```
